items.question_text: Under 5-7-1, speed adjustments should be kept to the minimum necessary and should avoid adjustments requiring alternate _____.
items.explanation: 5-7-1 states: avoid adjustments requiring alternate decreases and increases.

choices.text: decreases and increases

items.question_text: Under 5-7-1, when should speed adjustments be terminated?
items.explanation: Speed adjustments should be terminated when no longer needed to achieve or maintain required or desired spacing.

choices.text: When they are no longer needed to achieve or maintain required or desired spacing.
Only after the pilot has been issued a new altitude assignment.
After exactly 5 minutes of operation at the assigned speed.
Only when the aircraft has exited Class B airspace.

items.question_text: Under 5-7-2, which is NOT one of the methods for instructing aircraft on speed?
items.explanation: The methods include: maintain present/specific speed, maintain specified speed or greater/less, maintain highest/lowest practical speed, increase or reduce to a specified speed or by a specified number of knots.

choices.text: Maintain present/specific speed.
Reduce speed by 50 percent immediately.
Maintain the highest/lowest practical speed.
Increase or reduce to a specified speed or by a specified number of knots.

items.question_text: Under 5-7-3a, when assigning airspeeds to aircraft operating between FL 280 and 10,000 feet, what is the minimum speed?
items.explanation: 5-7-3a: between FL280 and 10,000 feet, assign a speed not less than 250 knots or the equivalent Mach number.

choices.text: Not less than 250 knots or the equivalent Mach number.
Not less than 210 knots.
Not less than 300 knots.
Any speed the controller deems appropriate.

items.question_text: Under 5-7-4, what phraseology is used when ATC-assigned speed adjustments are no longer required and no published speed restrictions apply?
items.explanation: 5-7-4a: when ATC-assigned speed is no longer needed and no published speed restriction applies, advise 'RESUME NORMAL SPEED.'

choices.text: RESUME NORMAL SPEED.
SPEED AT YOUR DISCRETION.
CANCEL SPEED RESTRICTION.
NO SPEED RESTRICTION.

items.situation: You need to assign a speed to an aircraft at FL200 for spacing. The pilot is VFR and not on an IFR flight plan. The aircraft is a jet that normally cruises at 240 knots at this altitude.
items.question_text: Under 5-7-3, what is the minimum speed you may assign?

choices.text: Not less than 250 knots or the equivalent Mach number.
240 knots, because that matches the aircraft's normal cruise speed.
210 knots, the minimum for turbojet operations.
No minimum applies because the aircraft is VFR.

items.explanation: 5-7-3A applies to aircraft between FL280 and 10,000 feet, requiring a minimum of 250 knots or equivalent Mach. At FL200, the aircraft is within this altitude band.
